Two questions. First topic is testimonials and endorsed mailings. You spend a lot of time on talking about controlling your message, what's said about you. I've heard you also speak about the fact that testimonials, when written by you, sound canned and contrived and it's better first person, but by the same token guiding them to get the message across you want. So how do you ask for what you want, get for what you want in testimonials and endorse mailings while having a nice mix of original thought and sound? Personally I am blessed in the sense that most of the testimonials I get are from people we have educated about good testimonials. Yeah, so I get a lot of good ones that aren't, that don't need doctoring. Can I have people I can get them from? Yeah. But I don't think they have any idea of what it sounds like. One answer is you got to show them samples, right? Some people make up little checklists of, you know, I don't want you to lie, but if any of these seven things are true we will, yeah. Sometimes you ought to just, if it's important enough to you and there's few enough of them, interview them by phone or in person. Get it all, get it transcribed, go back and tweak it and then have them, get them approved. Yeah. We just went through this with our corporate client, Bill and I are doing a lot of work for Miracle Air and they have 1200 stores, they're an hearing aid business and it actually frankly didn't occur to me that one of the problems we were going to face was no testimonials. Because there, first of all there's 1200 stores so you would think well hell everybody's got to have one, right? I mean so, so I got to be able to have 1200 to choose from, right? And I mean it's an emotional purchase so the testimonials should naturally be about grandkids and birds and you know. Well, for starters they like huh what testimonial you know then because they're not using any in their advertising. No, zero, zip, nothing. A few of the franchise owners including a couple who were already Glaser Kennedy and her circle members had started moving in that direction so they had like a testimonial book in their lobby and stuff but like nothing system wide. And then most of them had had fill out a testimonial questionnaire which they had cleverly constructed to get nothing but blindingly dull ordinary information. So the first thing they send me is all these filled out questionnaires, right? Which are you know how would you rank your experience at your miracle air center satisfactory or unsatisfactory. So satisfactory you know and the essay part of that is Marge was very nice to me. Well you know I'm kind of looking for you know I'm a retired you know rock musician whose hearing went because of playing back up for the Rolling Stones and until I got your thing I couldn't hear my grandkids you know I mean that's what I'm looking for. Well they have no idea, right? So it took us a lot of effort to get them with samples including I wrote fakes. I mean I wrote them and said here go find one you know and I mean we finally through an enormous amount of pain and struggle have arrived at about 30 to 35 you know I knew that they're there you know it's just nobody was doing the right things to get them. When you are actively involved in doing them that way you're right the trick is to avoid having them all sound like you wrote them. And so you got to like try and capture their voice when you take what interviewing maybe that helps yeah is there a difference between how a the purpose and sound of a testimonial versus the structure and message of an endorsed mailing for example. Not much I mean you really want I mean the only difference the only thing added to the endorsed mailing is sort of the way the guy gets into it. I've written to you and sent you this letter because you know we're both members of the Alps and I know like me you hum to hum to you know so there's got to be some entry into giving the testimonial and then you have to decide what kind of exit you want out of it on the endorsed mailing because that's everything from a lift note saying read all of his stuff that's enclosed sometimes actually into the PS I've arranged with Barry to give you a free half hour consultation because you and I are buddies but you got to read his stuff and call them right away so you can go all the way into the offer in the endorsed mailing. In some rare cases I've done the entire pitch redone in the voice of the endorser.
